verdict.headline: The world does not pass.
verdict.summary: Humanity has built much of the network required to deliver intelligence, but not a dependable bridge from an answer to equal real-world agency.

measuredSubstrate.label: Measured Enabling Substrate
measuredSubstrate.warning: This score covers five measurable or proxy dimensions. It is not an end-to-end capability score.

dimensions.label: Digital access
dimensions.summary: The network reaches further than meaningful use.

dimensions.label: Language and understanding
dimensions.summary: Education-language access is measurable; AI quality across languages is not yet benchmarked.

dimensions.label: Learning and knowledge
dimensions.summary: Participation and completion are stronger than foundational proficiency.

dimensions.label: Economic and physical capability
dimensions.summary: Basic infrastructure is widespread, while household protection remains thin.

dimensions.label: Institutional bridging
dimensions.summary: School connectivity is visible; the route from idea to mentor, laboratory, workshop or civic institution is not.

dimensions.label: Safety and rights
dimensions.summary: A framework exists, but no comparable global implementation measure exists.

dimensions.label: Agency and participation
dimensions.summary: No defensible global indicator currently measures whether a child can turn an idea into sustained action.

safeAiAccessGate.summary: The sampled leading consumer assistants do not provide one globally consistent, child-appropriate route for a six-year-old.

headlineCallouts.title: The signal reaches further than capability.
headlineCallouts.body: More than 96% are covered by mobile broadband, but 74% are online and only 58% of rural residents use the internet.

headlineCallouts.title: Access to an answer is not the same as the ability to use it.
headlineCallouts.body: At the end of primary school, 58% reach minimum reading proficiency and 44% reach minimum mathematics proficiency.

headlineCallouts.title: The physical world remains the bottleneck.
headlineCallouts.body: Electricity reaches 92% of people, clean cooking roughly 75%, while only 23.9% of children receive a child or family cash benefit.

headlineCallouts.title: We measure whether the school has internet. We do not measure where the child takes the idea next.
headlineCallouts.body: Only around half of the world's schools are online, and connectivity is still only a proxy for mentoring, tools, laboratories and civic support.

headlineCallouts.title: The world may fail before the answer begins.
headlineCallouts.body: The sampled leading consumer assistants do not offer one globally consistent route for an independent six-year-old user.

pathwayCheckpoints.label: Receives education in a language understood
Reaches minimum reading proficiency at end of primary
Receives a child or family cash benefit

pathwayWarning: These checkpoints use different populations, years and denominators. They are not a single cohort and must not be multiplied into an attrition funnel.
knownUnknowns: Whether an AI can produce safe, local, age-appropriate and achievable action plans across regions and languages.
Whether child-centred AI safeguards are implemented consistently in deployed systems.
Whether a child can reach a trusted adult, mentor, workshop, laboratory, public service or civic organisation.
Whether a child can influence decisions and sustain a real-world project after the first action.
